title: Four-Dollar Stackable Kids' Chairs
summary: A tiny, cheap kids' chair built from furring-strip 1x3s and 1x2s — slatted seat and back, sized to nest and stack for easy classroom or playroom storage.
description:
This chair is built to be made in multiples — the whole design leans on cheap furring-strip lumber and simple butt joints so a batch of four or six costs barely more than one. The front legs and side supports form the seat frame first; get that square before the back legs and slats go on, since everything above the seat references this frame's shape.

The back legs extend up past the seat to hold the back slats, spaced evenly — the plan suggests 1-1/2" gaps, but an arched cutout or swapping in narrower 1x2 slats both work as easy style variations if you want each chair to look slightly different.

The seat slats go on last with a small, deliberate 1/8" gap between each one, evenly spaced — that gap matters more here than on adult furniture, since a tight, dry-fit seat is what lets these chairs actually stack and nest for storage without the top slats catching on the chair below.

materials.name: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the seat frame
steps.body: Predrill and screw the side supports to the front apron with glue, using 1-1/4" screws or pocket holes.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws

steps.title: Attach the front legs
steps.body: Screw the front legs to the front apron and side supports with 1-1/4" screws.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws

steps.title: Add the side aprons
steps.body: Add the remaining side aprons with glue and 1-1/4" screws.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws

steps.title: Attach the back apron and legs
steps.body: The back apron is the same length as the seat slats — attach it with 2" screws and glue. Predrill and attach the back legs.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws

steps.title: Add the back slats
steps.body: Predrill and attach the back slats to the back legs, spacing 1-1/2" apart, or use an arched cutout for variety.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws

steps.title: Add the seat slats and finish
steps.body: Predrill two holes in both ends of each seat slat. Screw the seat slats to the chair frame with an even 1/8" gap between slats and glue at each joint. Sand and finish.
materials: Self-tapping screws, 1-1/4" and 2", or pocket hole screws
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Small stackable kids chair with a slatted seat and back
